hypothesis: If molecular generation is steered by adding concept-based activation vectors—derived from a Concept Bottleneck Model trained to learn orthogonal, property-specific embeddings in a frozen LLM's latent space—then the resulting molecules will more accurately satisfy specified physicochemical property targets (e.g., QED and LogP) compared to unsteered LLMs and alternative steering or fine-tuning methods, because the orthogonal concept embeddings provide disentangled, interpretable control over individual molecular properties without degrading the LLM's learned chemical knowledge.
